claim: Claude Design uses agentic context grounding where agents read source material (like design systems) before generating anything, rather than injecting everything into system prompts
actionable: Build agents that read and ground themselves in user data before generating outputs, implementing progressive disclosure of context based on task needs

claim: Structured memory in agents should be formatted in simple, portable formats (markdown, HTML, CSS, JSON) rather than proprietary schemas so downstream agents can consume it
actionable: Store agent-generated memory artifacts in standard formats (markdown/JSON) instead of custom schemas to enable reuse across different agents and systems

claim: Agents should use iterative refinement loops with multimodal input (chat, voice, DOM selection, drawing) to allow users to provide feedback in natural ways rather than forcing everything through text chat
actionable: Design agent UX with multiple input modalities (text, voice, visual markup) instead of single chatbot interface, allowing LLM to generate dynamic UI controls

claim: Self-QA loops where agents render their own output, take screenshots, and critique using vision models before human review can iterate autonomously until output matches intent
actionable: Have agents render/screenshot their outputs and use vision models to self-critique, iterating until quality matches intent before showing to users

claim: Multi-variation generation where agents surface high-level decision axes (like layout vs typography) and generate options upfront, forcing user buying-in on critical decisions before detailing
actionable: Have agents identify decision hierarchy and generate multiple variations along key axes upfront rather than single solution, letting users disambiguate intent early

claim: Agent outputs should be designed for handoff to other agents and tools using portable formats (HTML, CSS, JSON) that can export to multiple targets (PDF, PowerPoint, Figma, Claude Code)
actionable: Structure agent outputs in standard formats and design explicit handoff mechanisms to other agents and tools users already know

claim: The biggest unlock in Claude Design is combining context grounding with structured memory to inform all downstream patterns, replacing massive system prompts with dynamic context harness
actionable: Prioritize building dynamic context systems and structured memory first, then let all other agent patterns leverage that foundation instead of prompt-injection approach
